title: Cleistanthus robustus
intro: Cleistanthus robustus, also known as Tinospora cordifolia or Guduchi, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Cleistanthus robustus include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Cleistanthus robustus is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including stress, anxiety, and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Cleistanthus robustus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a type of shrub or small tree native to parts of Asia. The genus Cleistanthus is a part of the larger family Phyllanthaceae, which includes over 1,000 species. Cleistanthus robustus is further classified as a member of the order Malpighiales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf structure and flower morphology.
uses: Cleistanthus robustus has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. Studies have shown that extracts from the plant may have potential in treating various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant's medicinal uses are largely anecdotal and require further scientific investigation. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's efficacy and potential side effects.

constituents: Cleistanthus robustus contains a variety of active constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
